texto: Teach me how to love you so goodour hearts will be beatingthunderouslyagainst our ribcagesstraining to get out.For so long I have only knownhow to hurt.There are scars on my body likeconstellations. The one on my hip was from when I was sixand I learned my parents were the Titanic and the iceberg.My wrist has a faint bruisereminding me of when I gave myselfto a boy who crashed and burnedand took me down with him.Heartbreak sounds a lot likea slamming door.Show me it doesn’t have to be this way,I want to be proven wrong.Teach me how to love right.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Tina Tran sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “Teach me how to love you so goodour hearts will be beatingthunderouslyagainst our ribcagesstraini…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, heart, heartbreak, útil em momentos de dia-chuvoso e heartbreak.

frase_original: Teach me how to love you so goodour hearts will be beatingthunderouslyagainst our ribcagesstraining to get out.For so long I have only knownhow to hurt.There are scars on my body likeconstellations. The one on my hip was from when I was sixand I learned my parents were the Titanic and the iceberg.My wrist has a faint bruisereminding me of when I gave myselfto a boy who crashed and burnedand took me down with him.Heartbreak sounds a lot likea slamming door.Show me it doesn’t have to be this way,I want to be proven wrong.Teach me how to love right.

texto: I hear a small voicein the back of my mindand it is chanting a prayer:‘Please don’t fall in love again,please don’t fall in love again.’Maybe this time I will listen.Maybe this I will learn.
explicacao: Citação de Tina Tran sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “I hear a small voicein the back of my mindand it is chanting a prayer:‘Please don’t fall in love …” articula sabedoria, hurt, learn em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como learn e love.
